title: Backend Load Remediation - Location Analysis Fan-out and Similar Ranking
scope: location analysis request path, repository query shape, PostgreSQL pool pressure
problem: A single location-analysis request fans out into multiple DB calls, and the similar-location recommendation path fetches a broad joined dataset before scoring and trimming in Python.
risk: Under concurrent traffic, the location path can saturate the DB connection pool and shift heavy aggregation and ranking work into the API process.

evidence.note: Each analyze request performs get_sales, get_store_count, and get_similar_locations, with DB work running through thread offloading.
get_sales reads per-adm rows and aggregates in Python after fetchall.
get_store_count follows the same fetch-all then aggregate pattern.
get_similar_locations joins sales and store tables for an entire industry and performs filtering, outlier removal, normalization, and ranking in Python.
Repository pool maxconn is 20, so bursty request fan-out can exhaust available connections.

goals: Reduce DB calls and transferred rows per location analysis request.
Push ranking or pre-trimming closer to SQL where feasible.
Keep API worker CPU focused on orchestration rather than whole-industry scoring.

implementation_plan.action: Profile and trim the similar-location candidate set before Python scoring.
details: Rewrite get_similar_locations to aggregate in SQL and return only a bounded candidate pool.
Consider top-N by revenue, minimum store-count filters, or a capped candidate window before Python normalization.
Avoid fetchall over the effective full industry population if only top 3 outputs are needed.
Review whether get_sales and get_store_count can share a common adm_codes resolution and whether any aggregation can move into SQL summary queries.
Keep or strengthen in-memory caching, but do not depend on cache hits alone to make the path safe.
Log latency and candidate row counts for get_similar_locations.
Handle pool exhaustion cleanly and surface observability when the path becomes a hotspot.
If needed, lower concurrent thread offloading or sequence non-critical recommendation work after the primary analysis response path.

implementation_plan.action: Keep user-facing behavior stable while reducing work.
details: Preserve the current response contract for analysis, charts, and similar-location output.
If recommendation data becomes optional under load, fail soft for the recommendation section rather than the main analysis.

acceptance_criteria: Location analysis performs fewer or cheaper DB reads per request.
Similar-location ranking no longer requires loading the broad industry dataset into Python memory.
The endpoint remains functionally compatible with existing caller expectations.
Tests cover the recommendation path and no regressions appear in location-agent behavior.
verification: Run location-agent and repository-related test suites.
Add regression coverage for similar-location results shape and bounded candidate logic.
If possible, record before/after timings on representative analysis inputs.
execution_notes_for_claude_code: Prefer SQL-side bounding over simply increasing cache TTL.
If a full ranking rewrite is too large for one patch, land a safe intermediate cap first and document follow-up work.
